name: Watts et al. Synthetic Scientific Argumentation Inputs Extract
population_or_context: Middle-school scientific argumentation chatbot design and prototype evaluation.
sample_size: 18 synthetic student inputs and 162 chatbot responses across prompt versions are described in the source snippet.
methods: Extracted synthetic student input generation from prior rootworm-task responses, chatbot use cases, and Copilot prompting.
education_context: K-12 scientific argumentation around a rootworm task and chatbot support for claim-evidence-reasoning work.
response_origin: Synthetic student inputs were generated from high-quality and low-quality example student responses and typo/error conditioning.
rubric_label_space: High-quality versus low-quality scientific argumentation examples, plus typographical-error variants.
label_source: Prior researcher-classified student responses provide high-quality and low-quality examples for generation.
generation_method: Prompt-based generation of synthetic chatbot inputs from selected example student responses and articulated chatbot use cases.
prompt_strategy: Three sets: high-quality examples, low-quality examples, and mixed examples with instructions to include typographical errors.
evaluation_method: Synthetic inputs were used to test chatbot system prompt versions and evaluate generated chatbot feedback.
scorer_use: Prototype evaluation and prompt engineering for feedback generation; not automated scorer validation.
real_response_comparator: Example student responses from an earlier rootworm-task dataset condition the synthetic inputs.
reported_metrics: 18 synthetic student inputs; three independent chatbot responses per input for each version, totaling 162 chatbot responses.
limitations: The synthetic text is chatbot input rather than a scored assessment-response corpus.
Generation uses selected examples and use-case categories from one scientific argumentation task.
This source informs generation-method taxonomy more than scorer-validation evidence.
